title: Smart Pointers
description: Master unique_ptr, shared_ptr, and weak_ptr to enforce automatic memory reclamation in game engines.
learningObjectives: Implement std::unique_ptr for exclusive resource ownership
Use std::shared_ptr and std::weak_ptr for multi-owner designs
Break cyclic reference memory leaks with weak references

sections.title: RAII and Smart Pointers
sections.content:
Smart pointers are wrappers enforcing Resource Acquisition Is Initialization (RAII). They ensure dynamically allocated objects are destroyed automatically when they are no longer needed. 

1. `std::unique_ptr`: Sole owner of the allocated object. Zero overhead compared to raw pointers.
2. `std::shared_ptr`: Co-owner of the object. Maintains an atomic reference counter in a control block.
3. `std::weak_ptr`: Holds a weak, non-owning reference to break cycles.

sections.title: Unique Pointers in Scope
sections.content:
<< std::endl; }
};

class APlayer {
private:
    // player character holds exclusive ownership of this weapon instance
    std::unique_ptr<UWeapon> EquippedWeapon;

public:
    void EquipWeapon(std::unique_ptr<UWeapon> NewWeapon) {
        // Ownership is moved using std::move, transferring the raw pointer
        EquippedWeapon = std::move(NewWeapon);
    }

    void UseEquipped() {
        if (EquippedWeapon) EquippedWeapon->Attack();
    }
    // Destructor runs automatically, deleting the weapon pointer implicitly!
};
